title: oVirt Template and Snapshot Chain Depth Risk Analysis
app: REST inventory snapshot or Engine DB export job
spl:
index=ovirt sourcetype="ovirt:vm" earliest=-1d
| eval depth=tonumber(coalesce(chain_depth, snapshot_count))
| where depth>4
| stats max(depth) as max_depth, values(base_image_id) as bases by vm_name
| sort - max_depth
description: Deep snapshot chains slow reads and complicate backups. Identifying VMs over policy thresholds guides consolidation windows.
value: Reduces I/O latency surprises and shortens backup windows.
implementation: Schedule daily inventory. Alert when `chain_depth` exceeds internal standard. Automate emails to VM owners with merge instructions.
visualization: Histogram of depths; table worst VMs; trend over weeks.
grandmaExplanation: We keep an eye on the control panel that runs your virtual datacenter—who changed what, whether storage and hosts are healthy, and when something is about to strand running machines.
knownFalsePositives: AOS storage metrics can look worse during background heal, curator, or disk removal work; match alerts to Nutanix task progress and maintenance windows.
